messages.content: google sheet function to apply a timestamp to a cell whenever a reference cell value equals true. when false do not update the timestamp

You can use the IF function combined with the NOW function to achieve this in Google Sheets. Here's an example:

Assuming that cell A1 contains the reference value and you want to update the timestamp in cell B1:

1. In cell B1, enter the following formula:
=IF(A1=true(), NOW(), "")

This formula checks if A1 equals true. If true, it applies a timestamp using the NOW function. If false, it leaves the cell empty ("").

2. Format cell B1 as the desired date/time format. To do this, right-click on cell B1, select "Format cells", choose the format you prefer under the "Number" tab, and click "Apply".

Now, whenever the reference cell (A1) is true, the timestamp in B1 will update, but it will remain blank if the reference cell is false.
fix the previous code so that the timestamp in B1 doesnt go away if A1 is false

Apologies for the confusion. To ensure that the timestamp in cell B1 doesn't change when cell A1 is false, you can modify the previous formula as follows:

1. In cell B1, enter the following formula:
=IF(A1=true(), IF(B1="", NOW(), B1), "")

This modified formula checks if A1 equals true. If true, it applies a timestamp using the NOW function only if cell B1 is empty. If B1 is not empty, it keeps the existing timestamp. If A1 is false, it leaves the cell empty ("").

2. Format cell B1 as the desired date/time format (same as mentioned before). Right-click on cell B1, select "Format cells", choose the format you prefer under the "Number" tab, and click "Apply".

Now, the timestamp in B1 will update only if A1 is true and cell B1 is empty. It will remain unchanged if A1 is true but B1 already has a timestamp, and it will be empty if A1 is false.
fix the previous code so B1 will update the timestamp even if there already is one

Apologies for the confusion. If you want to update the timestamp in cell B1 even if there is already a timestamp present, you can modify the previous formula as follows:

1. In cell B1, enter the following formula:
=IF(A1=true(), NOW(), B1)

This modified formula checks if A1 equals true. If true, it applies the timestamp using the NOW function. If false, it keeps the existing value in cell B1.

2. Format cell B1 as the desired date/time format (same as mentioned before). Right-click on cell B1, select "Format cells", choose the format you prefer under the "Number" tab, and click "Apply".

Now, whenever the reference cell (A1) is true, the timestamp in B1 will update, even if there was already a timestamp present. If the reference cell is false, the content of B1 will remain unchanged.
keep this the same but have the now function use central daylight time

To apply the NOW function to use Central Daylight Time (CDT) in Google Sheets, you can combine the NOW function with the TIME function to adjust the time accordingly. Here's the modified formula you can use:

Assuming cell A1 contains the reference value and you want to update the timestamp in cell B1:

1. In cell B1, enter the following formula:
=IF(A1=true(), NOW() + TIME(-5,0,0), "")

In this formula, NOW() retrieves the current timestamp, and the TIME function adjusts it by subtracting 5 hours (CDT is 5 hours behind Coordinated Universal Time, or UTC). If the reference cell A1 is false, the formula leaves cell B1 empty ("").

2. Format cell B1 as the desired date/time format. Right-click on cell B1, select "Format cells", choose the format you prefer under the "Number" tab, and click "Apply".

With this modification, the timestamp in B1 will update to the current time using Central Daylight Time (CDT) when the reference cell (A1) is true. If the reference cell is false, B1 will remain blank.
